# MCP server allowlist

Single source of truth: [`docs/mcp.allowlist.yaml`](../mcp.allowlist.yaml). Last reviewed: **2026-05-08**.

Every code path that reaches an MCP tool must call `agentic_alz.mcp.assert_allowed(server, tool, mode)`. Adding a server in `mode: write` requires a NetSec CODEOWNER on the PR; the OPA policy [`policies/mcp_allowlist.rego`](../../policies/mcp_allowlist.rego) refuses any `write` entry without a `netsec_approval` block.

| Server id | Transport | Mode | Tools | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `azure-mcp` | `stdio` | `read` | `resources.list`, `resources.get`, `rbac.list_assignments`, `policy.list_compliance_states`, `monitor.query_logs` | Live inventory and RBAC reads, post-deploy polling. Used by Drift Triage and (future) firewall import. Never mutates Azure state. |
| `arm-mcp` | `stdio` | `read` | `deployments.what_if`, `policy.simulate` | What-if for ARM/Bicep templates and policy assignment simulation. Read-only by definition; cannot create or update deployments. |
| `terraform-mcp` | `stdio` | `read` | `registry.search_modules`, `registry.get_module_versions`, `registry.get_module_metadata` | AVM module discovery and version resolution for the Design stage. Never invoked at apply time. Output is cross-checked against `templates/<topology>/versions.lock`. |
| `microsoft-learn-mcp` | `stdio` | `read` | `search`, `fetch_doc` | Advisory citations for the Design stage and ADRs. Output is treated as **non-authoritative** for security-relevant decisions — reviewers verify URLs by hand. See `docs/threat-model.md`. |
| `github-mcp` | `stdio` | `write` | `pull_requests.create`, `pull_requests.update`, `pull_requests.add_comment`, `pull_requests.request_review`, `issues.create`, `issues.update`, `issues.add_comment`, `issues.add_labels`, `checks.list`, `checks.get` | PRs, issues, checks, and review requests only. The orchestrator may never use this server to push commits, edit workflow files, bypass branch protection, or approve a deployment environment. |

## NetSec approvals (mode: write)

| Server id | Reviewer | Date | Justification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `github-mcp` | @netsec-team | 2026-05-08 | The squad bootstrap and the Drift Triage stage need to open PRs and issues. Tool list is restricted to the PR/issue surface; `git push`, file edits via the GitHub Contents API, workflow dispatch, and environment approvals are deliberately excluded. Apply remains a CI job consuming an immutable saved plan artifact — `github-mcp` cannot trigger or edit it. |
